_meta.notes: Resource items — Phase 5 Day 20. Gathered from locations (Day 21), consumed by recipes (Day 22), bought/sold by vendors. Each entry: id (kebab-case slug), name (UI), description (1-2 lines, prose), category=resource, baseValue (vendor anchor; vendor.json overrides), rarity, tags (filter), sourceLocations (hint for gathering — resolved by content/locations/<id>.availableResources). All slugs are also `add_inventory.itemId`-compatible so the existing tool path works without changes.

items.name: iron ore
items.description: Dull, heavy, flecked with rust. The smelters know what to do with it.

items.name: copper ore
items.description: Greenish veins in cracked rock. Bends before it breaks.

items.name: silver ore
items.description: A cold, faintly luminous stone. Does not warm in the hand.

items.name: oak wood
items.description: Heavy, slow-growing, slow to burn. Good for handles.

items.name: pine wood
items.description: Soft, fragrant, drips amber. Easy to split.

items.name: emberleaf
items.description: Crushes warm. Smells faintly of a fire that hasn't been lit yet.

items.name: silverroot
items.description: A pale taproot. Tastes like cold iron and bitter winter.

items.name: wolf pelt
items.description: Coarse grey fur, black at the spine. Still smells of the kill.

items.name: bear pelt
items.description: Bigger than you'd think. Heavier. Warm to the touch even cured.

items.name: arc-crystal
items.description: A finger-length splinter that hums when held. Stings if bitten.

items.name: river clay
items.description: Cold, grey, clings to the hand. Holds shape until fired.

items.name: linen cloth
items.description: Plain weave, no embroidery. Functional. Cool in summer.

items.name: salt block
items.description: Heavy white slab. Heavy because it remembers being sea.

items.name: coal
items.description: Black, brittle, warm. Smudges everything it touches.

items.name: honeywax
items.description: Sweet golden block. Smells of summer two miles back.

items.name: iron ingot
items.description: Smelter-cast, plain bar. The smith's raw material.

items.name: copper ingot
items.description: Reddish bar, smaller than iron, easier to work.

items.name: silver ingot
items.description: Pale slab. Cold even when freshly cast.

items.name: oak planks
items.description: Squared, smooth, ready for joinery.

items.name: pine planks
items.description: Lighter than oak, splinters easier, faster to mill.

items.name: estuary pearl
items.description: Small irregular pearl from the freshwater mussels at the river's mouth. Cloudy lustre; not a luxury good but a recognized currency in Caelum's pidgin markets.

items.name: tidewater pearl
items.description: Brackish-water pearl with faint blue undertone. The Saltgale wholesalers trade them by the dozen.

items.name: Wash river reed
items.description: Long-stemmed estuary reed, mixed-water tolerant. The base fiber for Caelum's pidgin paper.

items.name: river reed
items.description: Common fresh-water reed used for paper-fiber and basket-weaving alike.

items.name: pidgin paper
items.description: Coarser than orcish paper, thicker, takes ink unevenly. The Caelum markets use it because nobody trusts a pristine sheet for a verbal deal.

items.name: loom iron
items.description: A soft iron the Coldhammers cast specifically for loom-weights. Rings differently from forge iron; the Threadwardens know which is which by sound alone.

items.name: cold-forged blade
items.description: A small knife or shaving blade hammered cold in the Threadwarden humid air. Edge holds for an unfair length of time; smiths elsewhere have tried and failed to replicate.

items.name: furrow grain
items.description: Generic single-strain human grain — wheat, barley, or rye depending on the farmer. Sold by the sack at the Furrowmouth market.

items.name: wheat flour
items.description: Milled at Furrowmouth on Wheat Day and shipped while it's still the same week. Goes stale fast; the elven wholesalers know the dates.

items.name: barley malt
items.description: Sprouted-and-dried barley. Trade good for distillers and bakers both. Furrowmouth ships it in waxed sacks.

items.name: rye meal
items.description: Coarse rye flour. Heavier and more sustaining than wheat. Furrowmouth's Rye Day is the noisiest of the three.

items.name: Late October (named cloth)
items.description: A Threadwarden bolt named for the light it catches. Each named cloth is unique; the master who wove it dies, the name retires.

items.name: estuary mud
items.description: Iron-tasting tidal silt. Raw material for mudglass and a usable wattle clay in a pinch.

items.name: mudglass prism
items.description: Estuary mud kiln-fired into translucent prismatic plates. The elven counting houses use it for window glazing; the light through it is famously honest.

items.name: saltgrass
items.description: Tough mudflat grass; the elven Salt-folk use it for thatching their pontoon roofs.

items.name: Saltgale vellum
items.description: Smooth, unwrinkled writing surface — the elven Vellum prefer it for contracts. Holds ink without bleeding.

items.name: glow-fly egg
items.description: Tiny phosphorescent egg laid in tidal mud at Mudmoth. Used as a marker dye in mudglass firing — three eggs per kiln.

items.name: tallow reed
items.description: Wax-fibered reed grown chest-high in the Tallowfen brackish marsh. The unrendered fiber smells faintly of bay.

items.name: tallow wax
items.description: Clean-burning candle wax rendered from tallow-reed fiber. Burns without smoke; the elven counting houses use nothing else.

items.name: fen bog-iron
items.description: Iron deposits forming naturally in the Tallowfen wetland. Soft, easily worked, stains everything orange. The cold-forge smiths refuse it; the loom-weight casters love it.

items.name: cloudtin ore
items.description: Surface-deposit tin found above the cloudline. The dwarves disapprove of mining it on principle, but they tolerate gathering loose surface ore — the line is fine and very Highfield-ish.

items.name: summit amber
items.description: Pale-yellow tree-resin amber from the highest orchard terraces. Sometimes contains a perfectly preserved Skybeam pollination bee.

items.name: cloudtea leaf
items.description: Pale grey-green leaf from the cloudtea bushes that grow only above the cloudline. Brews bitter, then sweet, then bitter again.

items.name: cloud-bitters
items.description: Clear blue tincture of cloudtea, painstakingly drop-distilled. The orcish Hush-readers take a single drop on the tongue at the start of each long reading session.

items.name: wild-bone drying frame
items.description: A drying rack made from goat-bone and wire. The cloudtea is laid across the bones to take up subtle minerals as it dries.

items.name: Whitestar apple
items.description: A heritage apple grown by a single Topgrove lineage for nine generations. White-fleshed, low-sugar, faintly metallic. Slices into perfect five-pointed stars.

items.name: Greybark pear
items.description: Small grey-skinned pear from the middle terraces. Tougher than its appearance suggests; ages well in storage.

items.name: Whitebark cider
items.description: Birch-cask aged cider. The dwarven drink. Sells in Caelum at three times the local price.

items.name: skybeam bone tool
items.description: A long-handled bone tool used to thread pollination kites. Carved from a single piece of mountain-goat femur.

items.name: switchback quartz
items.description: Clear quartz fragments common in the rubble of the northern switchback pass. Travelers pick them up as good-luck tokens.

items.name: mule-tail bone
items.description: Slim vertebrae from the climbing mules of Three Notches, kept and traded as luck-charms by the guides who survived a hard climb.

items.name: coralwhite aragonite
items.description: Dead-coral aragonite, harvested from the Anchorage's lower reef-shelves. Carves like ivory and is harder than it looks.

items.name: reef iridium
items.description: A rare iridescent metal found in trace seams along the dead-coral reef. The halflings collect it patiently and trade it expensively.

items.name: coral kelp
items.description: Kelp species that grows only on living coral, harvested by halflings without cutting the host. Edible after a long boil; essential to the salt-cured fish recipe.

items.name: salt-cured fish
items.description: The halfling provisioner staple — fish dried in coral-salt brine. Keeps for months; one fish is one halfling's dawn-meal.

items.name: saltcord rope
items.description: Knot's Landing flax-rope, salt-cured in the halfling Anchorage's brine. The standard naval rigging across the coast.

items.name: Drowncaller flag cloth
items.description: Heavy waxed canvas in the halfling colors. Made for the freelance navy's signal flags; the wax keeps it readable in any weather.

items.name: pageclay
items.description: Soft white clay from the high-desert plateau, mixed into orcish paper-pulp for clarity and weight. The Long Indices' signature paper depends on it.

items.name: ink onyx
items.description: Black onyx with veins so dark they swallow light. Ground into the orcish ink that doesn't fade in archive conditions.

items.name: inkwort
items.description: Plateau plant with deep purple sap. Pressed into the orcish ink for the dye component. Stains hands for a week.

items.name: reed paper
items.description: The orcish writing standard. Reed-fiber + pageclay + a slow press. Holds ink without bleeding for a century.

items.name: Marginal folio
items.description: A bound folio of pure marginalia — annotation upon annotation, no original text. The orcish answer to philosophy in book form.

items.name: vow poppy
items.description: White poppy grown in the Quietmile fields. Petals dried and shipped to the Long Indices for ink-binding; the poppy itself is mildly soporific.

items.name: vow-petal silk
items.description: A silken textile derived from layered vow-poppy petals. Used in orcish bookbinding for the very oldest restorations.

items.name: river flax
items.description: Flax retted in the slow Furrow waters. The Knot's Landing rope-makers won't use any other source.

items.name: knot-stamp clay
items.description: Riverbank clay used for the Knot's Landing rope-stamps. Picks up the namesake knot's pattern in a single press.

items.name: bell bronze
items.description: Briny Bell's local bronze alloy. Higher tin content than usual; the bells ring at a frequency that carries over wind and water.

items.name: mill-wood oak
items.description: Oak cut and aged for mill-wheel construction at Cataract Mile. Specifically resistant to constant water exposure.

items.name: green-claw crab
items.description: Small green crab from the rocky stretches of coast near Crab-by-Crab. Sweet meat; identifiable by claw-angle alone.

items.name: blue-back crab
items.description: Mid-sized crab with a steel-blue carapace, found in deeper sand pools. Saltier than the green-claw; preferred by halfling salt-keepers.

items.name: sand-runner crab
items.description: Long-legged sand crab, fastest on the open beach. The fishers chase them with low nets at first light.

items.name: broth bay-leaf
items.description: The single bay-leaf floated on every Crab-by-Crab broth bowl. Local-grown, not the Saltgale variety.
